evaluation_inputs.system_description: Wargame 2000 is a real-time, human-in-the-loop ballistic missile defense simulation system. It is designed to model concepts of operation, doctrine, and tactics in a realistic combat environment. The system supports distributed discrete event simulation, allowing for the integration of dissimilar parts and interoperability with other simulations via High Level Architecture (HLA).

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: Documentation existed in Rational Rose/UML but was considered weak for capturing discrete events. Some architectural drawings caused stakeholder confusion and required real-time refinement.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: How are discrete simulation events represented in the static UML models?
Can you clarify the data flow in this architectural diagram to resolve stakeholder confusion?
How does the code reuse strategy align with the need for high-fidelity discrete event simulation?

hardware_view_description: Distributed simulation environment capable of hosting online, offsite, and multiple simultaneous games.
Networked command centers allowing participants to play from their own locations.
runtime_constraints: Must provide 'Margin' (running faster than real-time) to support future requirements.

questions: How does the client-server separation impact the latency requirements for the human-in-the-loop displays?
How does the publish/subscribe mechanism handle peak load during high-density missile scenarios?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: Simulation controller initiates simulation execution, starts subordinate processes, and runs from T=-infinity to T=0.
Change joint firing doctrine rule set in the automated weapon target assigner in time for the next game.
Add cruise missile infrastructure into next software release.
Reconfigure Wargame 2000 from running a theater ballistic missile defense simulation to a national missile defense simulation in one week.
Replace existing database with an object-oriented database within 5-10 staff years.

business_goals: Answer the 'Big Five Questions' regarding operator knowledge and action timing
Serve as a rapid prototyping facility for interoperability studies
Support evolving mission needs and incremental functionality extensions
technical_constraints: Must be compliant with High Level Architecture (HLA) standards
Must run on government-owned contractor-operated infrastructure
Must support human-in-control experiments with 'fog-of-war' realism

architectural_approaches.description: Separates the event generator and user interface from the core system logic.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Allows independent upgrading of the Event Generator or UI without impacting the core.

architectural_approaches.description: Strategy used to generate and process simulation events.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Enables efficient processing of complex battle scenarios.

architectural_approaches.description: Decoupling mechanism used within the MADSIM subsystem.
Integration point for external simulations compliant with HLA standards.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Standardized interface for connecting to other DoD simulations.

children.scenario_description: Must perform all initialization activities within 10 minutes.
Run simulations with no instantaneous lags greater than five seconds.
Set-up and test time should be less than two weeks.
Build the graphical parameter file front end to allow user to click on an entity and change characteristics.
Run scenarios without crashes; no hardware/software failures.
Provide a virtual gaming site that includes online, offsite, and multiple games running simultaneously.

scenarios.scenario_text: Build the graphical parameter file front end.
Apply heavy data throughput to the GUI workstation and analyze performance.
Run an entire simulation without crashes; no hardware or software failures.
Simulation set-up and test time is less than two weeks.
A pause in the simulation occurs signifying a timing problem; identify the source.
Provides a virtual gaming site for distributed, simultaneous games.
A new post-processor is added that produces a summary report.
Be able to make a change to the simulation entity base class and understand the impact.

scenario_reference.text: Build the graphical parameter file front end. (This graphical set-up tool allows the user to click on an entity on the screen and change its characteristics).

architectural_decisions: Use of text-based parameter files for initialization
Deferral of graphical configuration tool development
analysis_records.reasoning: Text files allow fast machine initialization but slow human configuration. The lack of a GUI tool creates a bottleneck for frequent exercise turnaround.

risks.description: Inability to configure parameter files easily and consistently.
risks.rationale: Manual text editing is error-prone and slow, threatening the goal of one exercise per month.

risks.description: Poor configuration management of parameter files.
risks.rationale: Manual changes are not effectively tracked against automated generation tools.

scenario_reference.text: Provide a virtual gaming site that includes online, offsite, and multiple games running simultaneously and that allows participants to play from their own command centers.

analysis_records.reasoning: The standard mandates the interface but not the implementation performance. The current gateway implementation is suspected to be insufficient for real-time demands.

risks.description: HLA interface implementation may not meet performance expectations.
risks.rationale: Adherence to the standard does not guarantee the necessary throughput or latency for real-time gaming.

scenario_reference.text: Analysis of system stability and speed during high-load sensor initialization.

analysis_records.reasoning: Simultaneous initialization spikes CPU/Network load. Monitoring adds overhead.
